Container.Name: dns-monitoring
Container.Overview: DNS Monitoring - Self-hosted DNS query monitoring and analytics. Pre-built image from Docker Hub

Variable.Name: QUERY_RETENTION_DAYS
Variable.Description: Days to retain DNS query history

Container.Description:
DNS Monitoring - Self-hosted DNS query monitoring and analytics web application
Pre-built image available on Docker Hub: freemen810/dns-monitoring:latest
FEATURES: - Real-time DNS query monitoring and logging - React-based web dashboard with analytics - Express.js REST API backend - SQLite database for persistent storage - Query history and reporting - Fully configurable via environment variables - Production-ready deployment
QUICK START: 1. Add this container via the Unraid Docker template 2. Configure desired port (default 3000) 3. Set data volume path (default /mnt/user/appdata/dns-monitoring/data) 4. Adjust environment variables as needed 5. Click Apply to start
WEB ACCESS: - Default URL: http://[YOUR_UNRAID_IP]:3000
DATA PERSISTENCE: - Database: /mnt/user/appdata/dns-monitoring/data/dns-monitoring.db - All queries and analytics stored persistently
CONFIGURATION: - Port: Configurable in template (default 3000) - DNS Server: Configure DEFAULT_DNS_SERVER variable - 1.1.1.1 = Cloudflare - 8.8.8.8 = Google - 9.9.9.9 = Quad9 - Query Timeout: Adjustable via QUERY_TIMEOUT_MS - Retention: Set via QUERY_RETENTION_DAYS - Logging: Control via LOG_LEVEL
DOCKER HUB: https://hub.docker.com/r/freemen810/dns-monitoring
GITHUB REPOSITORY: https://github.com/freemen810/dns-monitoring
SUPPORT & ISSUES: https://github.com/freemen810/dns-monitoring/issues

Config.Name: Host Port
Config.Description: Web UI port (change to your desired port)
